topic.what: False Security Confidence (FSC) phenomenon where functionally correct LLM-generated code remains security-failing
topic.how: Defines FSC as conditional security-failure metric (security failure normalized by functionally-correct subset), proposes three-ecosystem task taxonomy, and introduces FSC-hard layer for statically-undetectable yet dynamically-exploitable cases
topic.why_matters: Quantifies over-trust risk in outputs counted as successful by conventional evaluation; changes measurement lens from threat-induction to prevalence-distribution of security failures within apparent success

characteristics.what_it_measures: Prevalence of security failure among functionally correct outputs
characteristics.evidence: Formal definition: |{s ∈ S : FCR(s)=1 ∧ SSR(s)=0}| / |{s ∈ S : FCR(s)=1}|
characteristics.context: Framework paper; no empirical numbers. Denominator excludes task-model pairs with zero functional correctness.

characteristics.what_itasures: Rate of outputs passing task-specific oracle tests
characteristics.evidence: Must be reported alongside FSC rate per paper recommendation
characteristics.context: Companion metric required for FSC rate interpretation

characteristics.what_it_measures: Proportion of FSC instances caught by static analyzers
characteristics.evidence: Paper posits significant portion evades static analysis (FSC-hard subset)
characteristics.context: Context for FSC-hard definition; static analysis treated as insufficient sole oracle

characteristics.what_it_measures: FSC instances undetected by static tooling but confirmed exploitable dynamically
characteristics.evidence: Defines FSC-hard as static-miss + dynamic-confirmed vulnerability subset of FSC
characteristics.context: Most deceptive region aligning apparent-success with apparent-tool-silence

novelty: Formal definition of False Security Confidence (FSC) as measurement concept
Conditional FSC rate metric normalizing security failure by functionally-correct subset
FSC-hard refinement layer for static-evasive yet dynamically-exploitable vulnerabilities
open_problems: Large-scale empirical FSC-rate quantification across models and languages
Validation methodology optimization for different task ecosystems
Benchmark suite design and construction for FSC-hard detection
Impact of model architecture and training on FSC profiles
